title: Contract Mechanical Engineer – Process Plant
description_raw:
Spencer Ogden are recruiting for a Contract Mechanical Engineer to join an EPC team working on a major process plant upgrade in Greater Manchester. This role is strictly an individual contributor / discipline engineer role — we are not looking for a project manager.

You will be responsible for the detailed mechanical engineering design of static equipment (vessels, heat exchangers, columns) and associated pipework on a brownfield modification project. Work will be conducted under ASME VIII and PD 5500, with deliverables including equipment data sheets, line lists, and 3D model reviews in PDMS/E3D.

What you will be doing day-to-day:
- Produce and check mechanical equipment data sheets, material selection reports, and vessel sizing calculations
- Review and approve supplier documentation (MDRs, ITP, FDRs) for static mechanical equipment
- Attend 3D model reviews at 30%, 60% and 90% IFC stages in PDMS/E3D
- Participate in HAZOPs and SIL reviews as discipline engineer
- Support construction team with technical queries (TQs) and site instructions

Requirements:
- BEng/MEng mechanical or chemical engineering
- 5+ years’ detailed design engineering experience on oil & gas, petrochemical, or chemical process plant
- Proficient in ASME VIII Div.1, PD 5500, and ANSI/ASME B31.3 piping code
- Experience with PDMS or E3D 3D CAD tools is essential
- CEng MIMechE preferred

Rate: £500–£550 per day depending on experience (IR35 status: outside)
Duration: 9 months
Start: 4 August 2026
Location: Manchester (on-site 4 days/week; 1 day work from home)
